title: Myasthenia Gravis Market Set to Surpass USD 16 Billion by 2036, Fueled by Rising Diagnosed Prevalence and Wave of Targeted Biologic Approvals | DelveInsight
author: Delveinsight
description: The myasthenia gravis market is projected to grow from USD 6 billion in 2025 to over USD 16 billion by 2036, driven by rising diagnosed prevalence, improved diagnostics, and an expanding pipeline of targeted biologic therapies including FcRn inhibitors, complement inhibitors, and CAR-T cell therapies. The market is currently led by argenx and UCB, with numerous emerging candidates in late-stage development expected to significantly accelerate growth.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: IMVT-1402 is highlighted as a strategically significant next-generation FcRn inhibitor with strong efficacy and favorable safety profile, with top-line results anticipated in 2027, positioning the company as a key player in the expanding MG market.

Descartes-08 is described as a potentially transformative CAR-T cell therapy with capability to address growing demand for safer, more durable treatment options, with Phase III enrollment on track as of November 2025.

Company has multiple candidates in development including FABHALTA (Iptacopan), a complement factor B inhibitor in late-stage development with Phase III data submission planned by 2027, and Remibrutinib, a selective BTK inhibitor with regulatory submission expected no earlier than 2028.

Gefurulimab, an anti-C5 albumin-binding bispecific antibody, has entered regulatory review phase with first major market submission completed, positioning it as a prospective new competitor in the gMG treatment landscape.

Currently a market leader in myasthenia gravis with robust product portfolio including VYVGART and VYVGART HYTRULO/VYVDURA, which is projected to generate the highest revenue among all therapies by 2036.

Market leader with strong product portfolio including RYSTIGGO and ZILBRYSQ; recent positive CHMP opinion in March 2026 for pre-filled pen version of zilucoplan further improves patient convenience and commercial footprint.
Company markets IMAAVY (nipocalimab-aahu), a marketed therapy representing the shift toward complement inhibition and FcRn blockade targeting core disease mechanisms.

Developing Pozelimab + Cemdisiran, a dual complement inhibitor combination in late-stage development, reflecting the evolving treatment paradigm toward targeting antibody production and B-cell activity.
Cladribine capsules (MAVENCLAD) is in the robust clinical trial pipeline for myasthenia gravis treatment, contributing to the expanding therapeutic options.

title: Generalized Myasthenia Gravis Market on the Brink of Change: 8 Promising Late-Stage Drugs Signal a New Treatment Era | DelveInsight
author: Delveinsight
description: The generalized myasthenia gravis (gMG) market is experiencing significant growth with 8 promising late-stage drug candidates in development. The gMG market across 7 major markets was valued at USD 5.9 billion in 2025 and is projected to grow at 9.1% CAGR through 2036. Key therapies in development include FcRn inhibitors, complement inhibitors, BTK inhibitors, and innovative CAR-T cell therapies from major pharmaceutical companies.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: IMVT-1402 is highlighted as a strategically important and clinically promising asset with potential to set new standards in FcRn inhibition with improved safety profile
Co-developer of IMVT-1402, positioned as a key player in advancing next-generation FcRn inhibitor therapies for gMG
Descartes-08 RNA CAR-T therapy is described as a promising first-in-class, durable cell therapy with potential to reshape the autoimmune neurology landscape
Developing three late-stage gMG therapies (FABHALTA/Iptacopan, Remibrutinib, and others), positioned as a major player in the expanding gMG treatment market
Gefurulimab has progressed to regulatory review stage with first market application submitted, positioning it as an emerging competitor in gMG treatment
MAVENCLAD (Cladribine) shows promising initial findings and has potential to emerge as a differentiated, patient-convenient oral treatment option for gMG
Telitacicept is first-in-class therapy targeting both BLyS and APRIL pathways, already approved in China for gMG, representing significant global milestone
Pozelimab + Cemdisiran represents innovative dual-acting first-of-its-kind approach to C5 inhibition currently in Phase III trials for gMG

title: Priovant Expands Brepocitinib Development Program with New Phase 2b/3 Trial in Lichen Planopilaris (LPP)
author: Na
description: Priovant Therapeutics announced enrollment of the first subjects in a Phase 2b/3 clinical trial of brepocitinib for lichen planopilaris (LPP), marking the fourth indication in the drug's late-stage development program. The company expects a potential FDA approval for brepocitinib in dermatomyositis in Q3 2026, with additional Phase 3 data expected in non-infectious uveitis and Phase 3 initiation in cutaneous sarcoidosis in H2 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: As the parent company of Priovant, Roivant benefits from the expansion of brepocitinib's development program across multiple rare disease indications. The anticipated near-term FDA approval in dermatomyositis and advancement of additional Phase 3 programs represent potential revenue-generating assets and demonstrate successful drug development execution.

title: New England Journal of Medicine Publishes Positive Phase 3 VALOR Trial Results of Brepocitinib in Dermatomyositis
author: Priovant Therapeutics / Roivant Sciences
description: Priovant Therapeutics announced that its Phase 3 VALOR trial of brepocitinib 30 mg met its primary endpoint for treating dermatomyositis, showing superior efficacy to placebo across multiple measures including muscle strength, skin disease, and corticosteroid reduction. The FDA has granted Priority Review with a PDUFA target action date in Q3 2026. Serious infections were elevated in the treatment group compared to placebo, though they resolved with medical management.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: As the parent company of Priovant, Roivant benefits from the successful Phase 3 results and FDA Priority Review of brepocitinib, a key pipeline asset. The positive clinical data supports the company's autoimmune disease development strategy and near-term commercialization prospects.

title: 9 Biotech Stocks Trading Far Below Fair Value With Up to 250% Upside Potential
author: David Wagner
description: Moderna settled a long-running legal dispute over COVID-19 vaccine technology, paying $2.25 billion to Roivant Sciences' Genevant unit and Arbutus Biopharma—less than the $3 billion expected. While Moderna stock surged 15.99% on the news, analysts suggest it now trades 22% above fair value after a 108% six-month rally. The article identifies 9 biotech stocks trading 27.6% to 64.6% below fair value with potential upside of 31.6% to 251.4%.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: While the settlement removes uncertainty and allows focus on new drug development, the stock has rallied significantly (108% in 6 months) and now trades 22% above fair value with analyst price targets 27% below current price, suggesting limited near-term upside despite positive settlement news.
Roivant's Genevant unit receives up to $2.25 billion from the settlement, which is a significant financial benefit and removes legal uncertainty for the company.
Arbutus receives settlement payment from Moderna, providing financial benefit and resolution of long-running legal dispute.
